title: Documentation of Diagonale for MATLAB
text:
Documentation of Diagonale for MATLAB
Toolbox for statistical data analysis
Software available at: GitHub repository
Cite as (APA style): Gallicchio, G. (2025). Diagonale [software]. Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.16808782
Note: this documentation is work in progress

Installation link
Analysis parameters decision tree link
Analysis parameters definitions coming soon
Tutorials coming soon

title: Diagonale Analysis Decision Tree
text:
How to choose analysis parameters in Diagonale.









DecisionTree


cluster_permutation

permutation parameters


cluster_plssvd

PLS-SVD parameters


cluster_ols

OLS parameters


cluster_clustering

clustering parameters


cluster_global

Global/Common
analysis settings



root

Start



global_seed

di_cfg.analysis.randomSeed
di_cfg.analysis.nIterations
(required for all)



root->global_seed





perm

di_cfg.analysis.objective
= 'permutationH0testing'



root->perm





boot

di_cfg.analysis.objective
= 'bootstrapStability'



root->boot





perm_h0

di_cfg.analysis.permuteUnit
= 'wholeObservation' | 'withinObservation'
di_cfg.analysis.p_crit
(default 0.05)



perm->perm_h0





feat1

di_cfg.analysis.
inferenceLevel
= 'feature'



perm->feat1





clus1

di_cfg.analysis.
inferenceLevel
= 'cluster'



perm->clus1





late1

di_cfg.analysis.
inferenceLevel
= 'latent'



perm->late1





feat2

di_cfg.analysis.
inferenceLevel
= 'feature'



boot->feat2





late2

di_cfg.analysis.
inferenceLevel
= 'latent'



boot->late2





feat1_test

di_cfg.analysis.
testingApproach
= 'empirical' | 'parametric'



feat1->feat1_test





ols_ref

di_cfg.analysis.OLS
(see OLS tree below)



feat1->ols_ref





clus1_cluster

di_cfg.analysis.clusterParams
(see clusterParams tree below)



clus1->clus1_cluster





clus1_test

di_cfg.analysis.
testingApproach
= 'parametric'



clus1->clus1_test





clus1->ols_ref





late1_test

di_cfg.analysis.
testingApproach
= 'empirical'



late1->late1_test





feat1_corr_fdr

di_cfg.analysis.
correction
= 'FDR'



feat1_test->feat1_corr_fdr





feat1_corr_maxT

di_cfg.analysis.
correction
= 'maxT'



feat1_test->feat1_corr_maxT





feat1_fdr

di_cfg.analysis.FDR_dimensions
(if correction='FDR')



feat1_corr_fdr->feat1_fdr





clus1_corr

di_cfg.analysis.
correction
= 'maxT'



clus1_test->clus1_corr





late1_corr

di_cfg.analysis.
correction
= 'maxT'



late1_test->late1_corr





plssvd_zsc

di_cfg.analysis.plssvdParams
.zscoringVec
(default [false, true])



late1_corr->plssvd_zsc





plssvd_sc

di_cfg.analysis.
subjectCentering
= 'noCenter' | 'center'
(latent + repeated only)



plssvd_zsc->plssvd_sc






feat2->ols_ref





late2->plssvd_zsc













di_cfg.analysis.dataStruct is required for all analyses. It must be a MATLAB table variable that describes the structure of your data—specifically, what each row of your Y matrix represents. At minimum, it should include an ‘observationID’ column. The table can include additional columns for grouping variables, subject identifiers, or other design factors.
TO DO: show how to create a dataStruct table with an example.










ClusterParams



root

di_cfg.analysis.clusterParams
(required for cluster inference)



adjacency

di_cfg.analysis.clusterParams
.adjacencyMatrix
(required)



root->adjacency





threshold

di_cfg.analysis.clusterParams
.clusterFormingPvalThreshold
(default 0.05)



root->threshold





cont_idx

di_cfg.analysis.clusterParams
.distance_continuous_index
(if continuous dims exist)



root->cont_idx





sph_rad

di_cfg.analysis.clusterParams
.distance_spherical_radians
(if spherical dims exist)
(default: 0.63 for 32ch, 0.36 for 128ch)



root->sph_rad




















OLS



root

di_cfg.analysis.OLS
(optional for feature/cluster inference)



variance

di_cfg.analysis.OLS
.varianceType
= 'equal' | 'unequal'
(default 'equal')



root->variance





standardize

di_cfg.analysis.OLS
.standardize
= true | false
(default false)
(z-scores predictor & outcome)



root->standardize





ranked

di_cfg.analysis.OLS
.ranked
= true | false
(default false)
(applies rank transform)



root->ranked






















objective
inferenceLevel
supported
testingApproach
correction options




permutationH0testing
feature
yes
empirical, parametric
FDR, maxT


permutationH0testing
cluster
yes
parametric
maxT


permutationH0testing
latent
yes
empirical
maxT


bootstrapStability
feature
yes
n/a
n/a


bootstrapStability
cluster
no
n/a
n/a


bootstrapStability
latent
yes
n/a
n/a







di_cfg.analysis.objective


required for all analyses
allowed values: permutationH0testing, bootstrapStability


di_cfg.analysis.inferenceLevel


required for all analyses
allowed values: feature, cluster, latent


di_cfg.analysis.dataStruct


required for all analyses
must be a ‘table’ variable


di_cfg.analysis.ignore_row


required for all analyses (default is to ignore nothing)
if provided: column vector with one value per row of Y


di_cfg.analysis.ignore_col


optional for all analyses (default is to ignore nothing)
if provided: row vector with one value per column of Y


di_cfg.analysis.randomSeed


required for all analyses (default is 42)
must be finite integer scalar


di_cfg.analysis.nIterations


required for all analyses (default is 5000)


di_cfg.analysis.verbose


optional for all analyses (default is false)
type: logical/boolean
controls whether debug/progress information is printed to console


di_cfg.analysis.figFlag


optional for all analyses (default is false)
type: logical/boolean
controls whether figures/plots are generated during analysis




The following parameters must be set if di_cfg.analysis.objective = 'permutationH0testing'

di_cfg.analysis.permuteUnit


required when di_cfg.analysis.objective = permutationH0testing
not used when di_cfg.analysis.objective = bootstrapStability
allowed values: wholeObservation, withinObservation
note: final validity depends on design code in di_validateH0


di_cfg.analysis.p_crit


required when di_cfg.analysis.objective = permutationH0testing (default 0.05)
not used when di_cfg.analysis.objective = bootstrapStability


di_cfg.analysis.testingApproach


required when di_cfg.analysis.objective = permutationH0testing
not allowed when di_cfg.analysis.objective = bootstrapStability
allowed values depend on di_cfg.analysis.inferenceLevel = ...:

'feature': testing approach can be empirical or parametric
'cluster': testing approach can be parametric only
'latent': testing approach can be empirical only



di_cfg.analysis.correction


required when di_cfg.analysis.objective = permutationH0testing
not allowed when di_cfg.analysis.objective = bootstrapStability
allowed values depend on inferenceLevel:

feature: FDR or maxT
cluster: maxT only
latent: maxT only



di_cfg.analysis.FDR_dimensions


required only when:

di_cfg.analysis.objective = permutationH0testing
di_cfg.analysis.inferenceLevel = feature
di_cfg.analysis.correction = FDR

not allowed in all other cases





di_cfg.analysis.clusterParams


required when di_cfg.analysis.inferenceLevel = cluster
optional but ignored when inferenceLevel is not cluster


di_cfg.analysis.clusterParams.adjacencyMatrix


required when di_cfg.analysis.inferenceLevel = cluster


di_cfg.analysis.clusterParams.distance_continuous_index


required when:

di_cfg.analysis.inferenceLevel = cluster
at least one dimension is continuous



di_cfg.analysis.clusterParams.distance_spherical_radians


required when:

di_cfg.analysis.inferenceLevel = cluster
at least one dimension is spherical



di_cfg.analysis.clusterParams.clusterFormingPvalThreshold


required when di_cfg.analysis.inferenceLevel = cluster (default 0.05)





di_cfg.analysis.plssvdParams


required when di_cfg.analysis.inferenceLevel = latent
ignored when di_cfg.analysis.inferenceLevel is not latent


di_cfg.analysis.plssvdParams.zscoringVec


required when di_cfg.analysis.inferenceLevel = latent (default [false, true])


di_cfg.analysis.subjectCentering


optional, only valid when di_cfg.analysis.inferenceLevel = latent AND design has repeated measures
allowed values: center, noCenter (default is noCenter)
note: if set to center in OLS-based analyses (feature/cluster), causes validation error
repeated measures designs typically have subjects appearing in multiple conditions




The following parameters apply only when di_cfg.analysis.inferenceLevel = 'feature' or 'cluster', which are powered by OLS regression under the hood.

di_cfg.analysis.OLS.varianceType


optional for OLS-based feature and cluster analyses
allowed values: equal, unequal (default is equal)
equal compares groups through standard Student’s t-test (homogeneous variance assumed)
unequal compares groups through Welch’s t-test (heterogeneous variance allowed)


di_cfg.analysis.OLS.standardize


optional for OLS-based feature and cluster analyses
allowed values: true, false as logical/boolean (default is false)
when false, uses raw values
when true, z-scores both predictor and outcome variables (standardized slopes equal correlation coefficient)


di_cfg.analysis.OLS.ranked


optional for OLS-based feature and cluster analyses
allowed values: true, false as logical/boolean (default is false)
when false, uses raw/standardized values (it yields Pearson correlation coefficients when used with di_cfg.analysis.OLS.standardize = true)
when true, rank transform both predictor and outcome variables (it yields Spearman correlation coefficients when used with di_cfg.analysis.OLS.standardize = true)
